# Master spec § 12 — 5-phase bootstrap pipeline

Operators MUST honor the chronological order. Per master spec § 12: *"Each phase must be completed and validated before the downstream phase is initiated."*

This doc is regenerated from `config/bootstrap/phases.yaml` on every invocation of `scripts/bootstrap/lib/render-phases-md.py`. The same YAML drives `sovereign-osctl bootstrap phases` (inventory) and `sovereign-osctl bootstrap run` (dry-run plan), so the doc, the inventory, and the executor can never drift.

## Phase table

| Phase | Name | Artifacts |
|-------|------|-----------|
| I | Minimal Trixie Base | 3 |
| II | Zen 5 Kernel Compilation | 3 |
| III | Storage Layer + DKMS (ZFS) | 7 |
| IV | Container + Network Edge Isolation | 6 |
| V | Tetragon eBPF + Guardian + State Fabric Mount | 6 |

## Phase I — Minimal Trixie Base

**Description.** Master spec § 12 Phase I — empty NVMe → debootstrap'd Trixie base

**Preconditions** (must hold BEFORE this phase runs):

- SAIN-01 box powered on with a Trixie netinst USB / PXE source available
- Both NVMe drives present and visible to UEFI (lsblk shows nvme0n1, nvme1n1)
- Operator has reviewed config/preseed/sain-01.preseed.example.cfg

**Postconditions** (state AFTER this phase completes):

- Base Debian 13 (trixie) userspace installed on the EFI + root partitions
- cloud-init has consumed the user-data and applied first-boot config
- SSH access available; operator can drive subsequent phases remotely

**Artifacts:**

- `scripts/build/01-bootstrap-forge.sh` *(build-step)*
- `config/preseed/sain-01.preseed.example.cfg` *(config)*
- `config/cloud-init/sain-01.user-data.example.yaml` *(config)*

## Phase II — Zen 5 Kernel Compilation

**Description.** Master spec § 12 Phase II + § 2 — kernel.org-stable, AVX-512 + Zen 5 tunings

**Preconditions** (must hold BEFORE this phase runs):

- Phase I complete (Trixie base is the build host)
- /mnt/kernel_forge (≥ 64 GiB tmpfs) mounted by 01-bootstrap-forge.sh
- GCC 14 + build toolchain installed

**Postconditions** (state AFTER this phase completes):

- Custom kernel .deb packages present under /var/cache/kernel-forge/
- Kernel signed if profile.kernel.signing enabled (SDD-015 posture honored)
- inputs_hash recorded in build-state.yaml for SDD-019 reproducibility

**Artifacts:**

- `scripts/build/02-kernel-fetch.sh` *(build-step)*
- `scripts/build/03-kernel-config.sh` *(build-step)*
- `scripts/build/04-kernel-compile.sh` *(build-step)*

## Phase III — Storage Layer + DKMS (ZFS)

**Description.** Master spec § 12 Phase III + §§ 3, 4.1 — ZFS native tank pool + tiered datasets

**Preconditions** (must hold BEFORE this phase runs):

- Phase II kernel installed and booted
- ZFS DKMS module compiles cleanly against the new kernel
- Operator has acknowledged the NVMe wipe (raid0 is destructive)

**Postconditions** (state AFTER this phase completes):

- tank pool active across both NVMe drives
- 3 tiered datasets mounted (state-fabric, ai-workspace, scratch) with recordsize/compression per SDD-017
- ARC max clamped per profile; nightly scrub timer enabled

**Artifacts:**

- `scripts/hooks/during-install/zfs-pool-create.sh` *(installer-hook)*
- `scripts/hooks/during-install/zfs-datasets-create.sh` *(installer-hook)*
- `scripts/hooks/post-install/zfs-arc-clamp.sh` *(post-install-hook)*
- `scripts/hooks/recurrent/zfs-scrub.sh` *(recurrent-hook)*
- `systemd/system/sovereign-zfs-arc-clamp.service` *(systemd-unit)*
- `systemd/system/sovereign-zfs-scrub.service` *(systemd-unit)*
- `systemd/system/sovereign-zfs-scrub.timer` *(systemd-timer)*

## Phase IV — Container + Network Edge Isolation

**Description.** Master spec § 12 Phase IV + §§ 4.3, 8 — Podman, VFIO 4090 detach, asymmetric net

**Preconditions** (must hold BEFORE this phase runs):

- Phase III complete (tank pool hosts the container storage driver)
- IOMMU enabled in UEFI (intel_iommu / amd_iommu kernel cmdline)
- 4090 PCI ID identified for vfio-pci binding

**Postconditions** (state AFTER this phase completes):

- 4090 detached from nouveau/nvidia, bound to vfio-pci for guest passthrough
- PRO 6000 owned by host nvidia driver
- Asymmetric network rendered (data vs control plane on separate VLANs)
- Podman storage driver pointed at the tank ai-workspace dataset

**Artifacts:**

- `scripts/hooks/post-install/vfio-bind-3090.sh` *(post-install-hook)*
- `scripts/hooks/post-install/network-vlan-config.sh` *(post-install-hook)*
- `scripts/network/render-asymmetric.sh` *(tooling)*
- `systemd/system/sovereign-vfio-bind.service` *(systemd-unit)*
- `systemd/system/sovereign-network-vlan.service` *(systemd-unit)*
- `systemd/system/sovereign-nvidia-driver-bind.service` *(systemd-unit)*

## Phase V — Tetragon eBPF + Guardian + State Fabric Mount

**Description.** Master spec § 12 Phase V + §§ 5, 6, 10, 21 — kernel BPF, guardian, atomic state

**Preconditions** (must hold BEFORE this phase runs):

- Phase IV complete (network + GPU isolation enforced)
- bpf() syscall enabled; CAP_BPF available in the policy-loader unit
- operator-signed tetragon policy present in /etc/sovereign/tetragon/

**Postconditions** (state AFTER this phase completes):

- Tetragon kernel BPF policies loaded; sovereign-os events flowing to journal
- sovereign-guardian-core service running; auditing observed in JSONL log
- State fabric (state-fabric ZFS dataset) mounted with copies=2 redundancy
- System reaches master spec § 22 lock-state (verify.sh exits 0)

**Artifacts:**

- `scripts/hooks/post-install/tetragon-policy-load.sh` *(post-install-hook)*
- `scripts/hooks/recurrent/tetragon-policy-verify.sh` *(recurrent-hook)*
- `scripts/auditor/guardian-core.py` *(tooling)*
- `systemd/system/sovereign-guardian-core.service` *(systemd-unit)*
- `systemd/system/sovereign-tetragon-policy-load.service` *(systemd-unit)*
- `scripts/weaver/atomic-state.py` *(tooling)*

## Cross-references

- `sovereign-osctl bootstrap phases` — inventory (artifact-presence check)
- `sovereign-osctl bootstrap run --phase N` — dry-run execution plan
- `sovereign-osctl bootstrap verify` — master spec § 22 6-check operational grid (LIVE node)
- `config/bootstrap/phases.yaml` — canonical source consumed by every surface above
